title: bromus racemosus subsp. lusitanicus
intro: Bromus racemosus subsp. lusitanicus, also known as Portuguese brome, is a subspecies of the Bromus racemosus plant, a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Bromus racemosus subsp. lusitanicus include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and even help to lower blood sugar levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, and it can also be used in salves and creams to promote skin health and wound healing.
